decks.notes: The deck revolves around using artifacts and combo pieces to disrupt the opponent's strategy while assembling the combo of Painter's Servant and Grindstone to mill the opponent's library. Karn, the Great Creator and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain provide additional support and ways to control the board, while Urza's Workshop and Urza's Saga help ramp up the mana needed to execute the combo. Mishra's Research Desk and The One Ring provide additional utility and card advantage, making this deck a powerful and versatile option in Legacy 1 vs 1 format.

The deck aims to quickly assemble a combo that wins the game in a single explosive turn by milling almost the entire deck and then using Thassa's Oracle to win the game with no cards left in the library. The deck includes various fast mana acceleration cards like Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide, along with cards like Undercity Informer and Balustrade Spy to facilitate the combo. It also includes protection in the form of Pact of Negation and disruption with cards like Cabal Therapy. The goal is to win as quickly and efficiently as possible.

The main concept of the deck is to control the opponent's actions by disrupting their hand with cards like Thoughtseize and Daze, while also using removal spells like Fatal Push and Brazen Borrower to deal with threats. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Murktide Regent serve as strong finishers. The deck also utilizes efficient card draw and deck manipulation with cards like Ponder, Brainstorm, and Mishra's Bauble to maintain tempo and outpace the opponent. Wasteland and Force of Will help to protect the player's resources and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on locking down the opponent through the use of artifacts like Trinisphere and Planar Nexus, while also ramping up mana with Urza's lands and Grim Monolith. Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms provide control over the battlefield, while Candelabra of Tawnos and Voltaic Key help to untap artifacts for additional synergy. The goal is to establish a prison-like board state where the opponent is unable to effectively play their cards, while the player builds towards a powerful win condition like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn.

The main focus of the Dimir Tempo deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with efficient creatures and disruptive spells. By utilizing disruptive spells like Thoughtseize, Daze, and Force of Will, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and protect its own threats. Meanwhile, efficient creatures like Nethergoyf and Murktide Regent put pressure on the opponent's life total and provide a clock to close out the game quickly. This deck aims to control the game by disrupting the opponent's plays while maintaining a strong board presence.

The deck focuses on using lands as a resource to control the game, utilizing cards like Wasteland, Dark Depths, and Thespian's Stage to disrupt the opponent's strategy and generate value. It also includes cards like Life from the Loam and Exploration to ensure a steady stream of land drops and card advantage. The combination of land destruction, land manipulation, and powerful land-based threats allows the deck to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents over the course of a long game. The deck aims to establish a dominant board presence and control the game until it can secure a victory.

This Mardu Aggro deck focuses on using aggressive creatures like Amped Raptor and Guide of Souls to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It includes disruption cards like Thoughtseize and Cabal Therapy to control the opponent's hand, while also utilizing removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. The deck also features sacrifice outlets like Goblin Bombardment and Orcish Bowmasters for added versatility. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah serves as a powerful finisher, while cards like Wasteland and Karakas provide additional utility. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts such as Emry, Lurker of the Loch, Mox Opal, and Urza's Bauble to generate a lot of value and draw cards. It aims to control the board with cards like Goblin Welder and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain while digging for combo pieces like Agatha's Soul Cauldron and Legion Extruder. The deck also has a strong engine with Urza's Saga, allowing for consistent tutoring. The key strategy is to assemble combos quickly while disrupting opponents with artifact destruction and control elements.

The deck is focused on locking down the opponent's resources through the use of artifacts and control cards. It aims to establish a prison-like environment where the opponent is unable to play their spells effectively. By utilizing cards like The One Ring, Karn, the Great Creator, and Trinisphere, the deck can slow down the opponent's game plan while setting up powerful late-game threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. The combination of artifact ramp and disruptive elements make it difficult for the opponent to mount a successful offense, allowing the deck to control the game and secure victory.

This deck aims to control the game through a combination of lands that disrupt the opponent and generate value. Using cards like Crop Rotation, Life from the Loam, and Mox Diamond, the deck can quickly assemble powerful land combinations like Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage. It also includes disruptive elements like Sphere of Resistance and Pithing Needle to slow down the opponent's plan. By using lands creatively and efficiently, this deck can outlast opponents and grind out victories.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize artifact synergy and fast mana acceleration to quickly deploy threats and disrupt the opponent's game plan. By combining powerful artifact creatures like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Pinnacle Emissary with powerful artifacts like Urza's Saga and Chalice of the Void, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive game plan. Additionally, cards like Thoughtcast and Thought Monitor provide card advantage to keep the pressure on the opponent while cards like Force of Will provide protection against opposing threats.

This Legacy 1 vs 1 deck focuses on quickly playing low-cost spells and creatures to maintain tempo and pressure the opponent. The deck includes a mix of card draw, removal, and counter spells to control the board while playing aggressive creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent. By using cards like Force of Will and Wasteland, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while swiftly closing out the game with efficient threats. The versatile nature of the deck allows for adaptability in different matchups and situations.

This Show and Tell combo deck aims to quickly cheat out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier onto the battlefield using cards like Sneak Attack and Show and Tell. With the help of Omniscience, the deck can cast spells for free, overwhelming the opponent with powerful threats. The deck also includes various counterspells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect the combo pieces. The goal is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while quickly assembling the combo to win the game.

The main focus of this deck is to establish control over the game through a combination of removal spells, counter magic, card draw, and threats like Murktide Regent. By using cards like Swords to Plowshares and Prismatic Ending to deal with opponent's threats, along with counterspells like Force of Will and Force of Negation to protect your own game plan, this deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student acts as card advantage engine, while cards like Brainstorm and Ponder help set up the perfect draws. This deck seeks to maintain control of the game and win through incremental advantage.

This aggressive burn deck focuses on quickly dealing damage to the opponent through efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, as well as direct burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Chain Lightning. The deck also includes cards like Mishra's Bauble and Urza's Bauble for additional card draw and prowess triggers. With a low mana curve and plenty of burn spells, this deck aims to end the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize, utilizing both creatures and direct damage to secure victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing control elements and lands to disrupt the opponent while building up a powerful board presence. The key strategy involves using cards like Life from the Loam, Exploration, and Crop Rotation to quickly assemble powerful land combinations such as Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage. By disrupting the opponent's resources with cards like Wasteland and Boseiju, Who Endures, the deck aims to create a lockout situation where the opponent is unable to respond effectively. The inclusion of disruptive spells like Malevolent Rumble and disruptive artifacts like Grafdigger's Cage further enhances the deck's control strategy.
